mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real GDP growth reduces recession risk and boosts corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid tightening of the federal funds rate increases borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or t-test p-value exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll growth and stable unemployment indicate a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative Rank IC or forward returns do not differ significantly from baseline across any 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: High housing starts combined with elevated residential investment signal strong construction sector momentum and wealth effects, boosting equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict returns when housing starts exceed 1600 but residential investment declines concurrently, or IC drops below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and mining output, providing immediate positive momentum for equity markets over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no correlation with INDPRO month-over-month changes, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces anticipated monetary tightening, depressing equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite accelerating inflation above the threshold, or forward returns show no significant decline post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth coupled with stable inflation indicates healthy demand-pull expansion without overheating, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative returns when GDP growth exceeds trend but inflation spikes simultaneously, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high monthly payroll additions signal robust economic activity and earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days specifically during periods of labor market tightness.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat when payroll growth exceeds 200k but wage growth stagnates, or trigger fails to outperform baseline in walk-forward tests.
